title: Mapo Tofu Vegetarian

seo.title: Vegetarian Mapo Tofu Recipe - Authentic & Easy!
seo.metaDescription: Craving spicy, flavorful Chinese food? Try our easy Vegetarian Mapo Tofu recipe! Learn how to make this authentic dish without meat, perfect for any night. Get the recipe now!
longTailKeywords: how to make vegetarian mapo tofu
lowCompetitionKeywords: spicy vegetarian mapo tofu without meat
mapo tofu with silken tofu vegetarian
h2Headings: Tips for the Best Vegetarian Mapo Tofu
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of spicy, authentic Vegetarian Mapo Tofu with rice and green onions, ready to be served.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, spicy, and savory flavors of this incredible Vegetarian Mapo Tofu, a perfect meatless Chinese dish.

description: Experience the fiery and numbing delight of Mapo Tofu, reimagined for a vegetarian palate. This classic Sichuan dish features silky soft tofu bathed in a rich, spicy sauce, bursting with the authentic flavors of fermented bean paste and Sichuan peppercorns. It is a hearty and incredibly satisfying meal that brings the heart of Chinese cuisine right to your table.

ingredients.notes: white and green parts separated, thinly sliced
mixed with 3 tbsp cold water for slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Tofu and Aromatics
instructions.text: Gently drain the tofu and cut it into 1-inch cubes. Bring a pot of lightly salted water to a boil, then carefully add the tofu cubes and blanch for 2-3 minutes. This step strengthens the tofu and helps it absorb flavor. Drain the blanched tofu and set aside. Finely mince the fresh ginger and garlic. Separate the white and green parts of the scallions, thinly slicing both.

instructions.title: Infuse Oil with Sichuan Peppercorns
instructions.text: Heat 3 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the whole Sichuan peppercorns and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until they become fragrant and slightly smoky. Using a slotted spoon, remove and discard the peppercorns, leaving behind the aromatic oil. If using ground Sichuan peppercorns, you will add them in a later step.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Pastes
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Add the white parts of the sliced scallions, minced ginger, and minced garlic to the infused oil. Stir-fry for about 1 minute until fragrant. Add the doubanjiang and the rinsed, chopped fermented black beans. Continue to stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, pressing the paste against the wok to release its flavors, until the oil turns a rich red color and a deep aroma develops.

instructions.title: Deglaze and Simmer Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully deglaze the wok with Shaoxing wine (if using), scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Pour in the vegetable broth, light soy sauce, and granulated sugar. Stir everything together and bring the sauce to a gentle simmer. If you are using ground Sichuan peppercorns instead of whole, stir them into the sauce now.

instructions.title: Add Tofu and Thicken
instructions.text: Gently add the blanched tofu cubes to the simmering sauce. Stir very carefully to ensure the tofu is coated without breaking it apart. Let the tofu simmer in the sauce for 5-7 minutes, allowing it to fully absorb the rich flavors. Whisk together the cornstarch and cold water to create a slurry. Slowly pour the cornstarch slurry into the simmering sauce, stirring continuously, until the sauce thickens to your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the green parts of the sliced scallions. Taste the sauce and adjust seasoning if necessary, adding a pinch more salt or sugar as needed. For an extra kick and glossy finish, drizzle with a teaspoon of chili oil if desired. Serve the Vegetarian Mapo Tofu immediately over freshly steamed white rice, garnished with additional green onions and a final sprinkle of ground Sichuan peppercorns for an authentic numbing sensation.

tips: Blanching the tofu in salted water prior to adding it to the sauce helps it firm up slightly, preventing it from breaking easily, and also opens its pores to better absorb the flavorful sauce.

For authentic Mapo Tofu flavor, do not skip the doubanjiang (Sichuan chili bean paste) and Sichuan peppercorns. These two ingredients are crucial for the dish's signature spicy and numbing (mala) profile.
Serve Mapo Tofu immediately with plain steamed white rice. The rice helps to balance the intense flavors and spiciness of the dish, making for a truly satisfying meal.
article: Mapo Tofu Vegetarian is a beloved Chinese Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
